title: AI Prompt Engineering
public_claim: AI Prompt Engineering is presented as an AI assurance and retrieval-integrity instrument.
distinctive_mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from threat model to test condition, system behavior, error mode, human-review checkpoint, scoring rule, and release or rejection gate. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.
use_value: It shows the utility of AI Prompt Engineering by tying the project purpose to the mechanism that makes it inspectable, reusable, or professionally valuable.

component_proofs.verification_check: Open or inspect the local runtime path for AI Prompt Engineering Runtime Or Interface Surface; confirm a room, action, or state record traces to the governing contract.

component_proofs.component: AI Prompt Engineering Data And State Model
component_proofs.claim_supported: George and Bennett. Loads case-study design, process tracing, and theory-development discipline. Allison and Zelikow. Loads decision-model plurality: rational actor, organizational process, and bureaucratic politics. Jervis. Loads perception, misperception.
component_proofs.input: records, files, indexes, tables, project state, source state, and user decisions
component_proofs.expected_output: state model that explains what the tool remembers and what it refuses to infer
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: state model that explains what the tool remembers and what it refuses to infer
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (records, files, indexes, tables, project state, source...) produces output (state model that explains what the tool remembers...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

minimum_public_demo: Open or inspect the local runtime path for AI Prompt Engineering Runtime Or Interface Surface; confirm a room, action, or state record traces to the governing contract.
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
